programs.title: Push / Pull / Legs — 6 day
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: Push/pull/legs twice through the week at full session length. The high-volume default.

programs.title: Upper / Lower — 4 day
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: The most time-efficient split for most lifters — each muscle trained twice a week.

programs.title: Full Body — 3 day
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: High frequency on the big lifts across three sessions. The best place to start.

programs.title: PHUL — power & hypertrophy
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: Two heavy days, two volume days. Strength and size without choosing between them.

programs.title: Arnold Split — 6 day
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: Chest/back, shoulders/arms, legs — twice over. A lot of volume for advanced lifters.

programs.title: Hybrid Strength + Size
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: Heavy compound work early in the week, hypertrophy volume late.

programs.title: Bodyweight & Bands
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: Full-body training with one set of resistance bands. No gym, no weights.

programs.title: Garage Gym
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: Barbell, bench, dumbbells and a pull-up bar — the standard home setup, run as a proper upper/lower.

programs.title: Dumbbell Upper / Lower — 2 day
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: Whole body across two sessions. The minimum viable week when time is the constraint, not equipment.

programs.title: One Pair of Dumbbells
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: No bench, no rack. Floor presses, goblet squats and rows covering every muscle group.

programs.title: Dumbbell Push / Pull / Legs
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: PPL on a home kit — dumbbells and an adjustable bench.

programs.title: PPL — hamstring emphasis
programs.author: pursuit
programs.note: PPL with three swaps for a lifter with tight hips: less deep hip flexion, more direct hamstring work.
